HTTP Methods and Status Codes

HTTP Methods

  • GET — Retrieve information from the server.
  • POST — Send data to the server to create a new resource.
  • PUT — Update an existing resource completely.
  • DELETE — Remove a resource from the server.

Steps to Make an HTTP Request

  1. User enters a URL in the browser.
  2. The browser looks up the domain using DNS.
  3. The browser sends an HTTP request to the server.
  4. The server processes the request.
  5. The server returns an HTTP response.
  6. The browser displays the response to the user.

Advantages of HTTPS

  1. Encrypts communication between client and server.
  2. Protects sensitive information such as passwords.
  3. Improves website security and trust.
  4. Helps prevent cyber attacks.

Key Terms Definitions

URL
The address used to access resources on the internet.
DNS
A system that translates domain names into IP addresses.
Server
A computer or system that provides services and resources.
Client
A device or application that requests services from a server.

Nested Lists Example

  • HTTP Request Components
    • Method
    • Headers
    • Body
    • URL
  • Status Code Categories
    • 2xx — Success
    • 3xx — Redirection
    • 4xx — Client Errors
    • 5xx — Server Errors
